title: agent-runtime: background process gaps - state transitions, signals, backpressure
summary: Create and execute a current-state-aware implementation workflow for issue #593. The workflow must read the issue body, comments, and labels; perform a Phase 0 reuse audit; trace live runtime call paths; verify what staging already implements; then implement only remaining background process lifecycle gaps with tests, docs, compatibility review, and final spec guard.

reportedGaps: Missing paused/resumed and zombie/stale lifecycle states; cannot distinguish graceful exit from crash.
SIGTERM is not forwarded to child processes and graceful shutdown coordination is incomplete.
stdout/stderr retention can create memory pressure unless bounded and surfaced with explicit metadata.
Background processes are independent and lack dependency ordering.
No pre/post spawn, pre/post destroy, or timeout lifecycle hook contract.
Daemon start/stop exists, but session pause/resume semantics are distinct and not modeled as background process pause/resume.
triageNotes: Adjacent surfaces include daemon/lifecycle.ts, daemon/loop.ts, execution/modes/local.ts, and session/types.ts.
Related but non-duplicate issue #600 covers deduplicating background registry and shell invocation.
Related runtime gap issues #585, #590, #591, and #592 should be inspected only for scope boundaries.

currentStagingObservations.backgroundRegistry: packages/agent-runtime/src/backgroundProcessRegistry.ts currently exists and already includes lifecycle status values for paused, queued, timed-out, skipped, and hook-failed in addition to existing terminal states.
currentStagingObservations.streamBackpressure: The registry currently exposes stdoutTruncated, stderrTruncated, stdoutRetainedBytes, stderrRetainedBytes, stdoutDroppedBytes, and stderrDroppedBytes metadata backed by capped retention.
currentStagingObservations.signalHandling: The registry currently has process-group/direct-child signal paths and grace-period SIGKILL escalation, while LocalExecutor and daemon lifecycle retain separate direct process stop behavior.
currentStagingObservations.pauseResume: The registry currently exposes pause() and resume() with platform gating; daemon/session pause semantics remain adjacent and should not be conflated unless deliberately scoped.
currentStagingObservations.dependenciesAndHooks: The registry currently has dependsOn queueing, duplicate dependency validation, lifecycle hooks, and hook failure recording in the registry-focused tests.
currentStagingObservations.tests: packages/agent-runtime/src/__tests__/backgroundProcessRegistry.test.ts currently contains focused tests for output metadata, signal escalation, pause/resume, dependencies, and hooks.

reuseAuditSeed.newInfrastructureConstraint: Do not introduce a competing process manager. Extend or verify existing registry, executor, daemon, and docs surfaces only when the runtime trace proves they are live scope for issue #593.

plannedPhases.name: Issue spec read
plannedPhases.purpose: Read issue #593 body, comments, labels, docs/agent-layer-gaps.md, and related issues enough to lock source-of-truth scope.

plannedPhases.name: Reuse and remaining-gap audit
plannedPhases.purpose: Classify each issue requirement as already implemented, partially implemented, missing, unclear, or out of scope before proposing changes.

plannedPhases.name: Runtime call-path trace
plannedPhases.purpose: Trace live paths through registry spawn, stream retention, completion, cancel, killAll, dispose, pause, resume, dependencies, hooks, LocalExecutor destroy, daemon lifecycle, and daemon loop queueing.

plannedPhases.name: Acceptance matrix
plannedPhases.purpose: Map every issue requirement to source text, implementation files, tests, docs, and pass/fail rules.

plannedPhases.name: Regression plan
plannedPhases.purpose: Author or confirm tests before production edits, preferring existing BackgroundProcessRegistry tests and fake process seams.

plannedPhases.name: Plan review
plannedPhases.purpose: Challenge duplicate infrastructure, compatibility risk, platform behavior, hook/dependency deadlocks, and scope expansion.

plannedPhases.name: Remaining-gap implementation
plannedPhases.purpose: Implement only missing or partial issue #593 behavior on traced live paths, or harden tests/docs for behavior already present.

plannedPhases.name: Verification loop
plannedPhases.purpose: Run focused and package-level validation, then iterate up to three attempts on failing criteria.

plannedPhases.name: Compatibility and scope review
plannedPhases.purpose: Review public API changes, race risks, platform gating, documentation claims, and boundaries with adjacent runtime issues.

plannedPhases.name: Final spec guard
plannedPhases.purpose: Compare issue text to final artifacts and command results before accepting completion.

breakpoints.when: Only if a public lifecycle API decision, unsupported-platform behavior, or scope expansion into daemon crash recovery/durable queues needs maintainer input.
Only if final acceptance requires explicit deferral of an in-scope issue requirement.

qualityGates: Phase 0 reuse-audit findings are rendered before implementation recommendations.
Each issue requirement is classified against current staging before code changes.
runtimeCallPaths are recorded and implementation edits stay on traced live paths.
Tests are added or confirmed before production edits for every remaining or partially implemented criterion.
Stream retention tests prove bounded retained bytes and surfaced dropped/truncated metadata for stdout and stderr snapshots plus completion events.
Signal tests prove process-group or direct-child target selection, grace timeout, escalation, and idempotency without hanging.
Lifecycle tests prove valid status transitions, timeout behavior, pause/resume platform gating, dependency ordering/skip behavior, and hook failure recording.
Docs claims are updated only for behavior actually implemented and verified.
Compatibility review approves public API changes and rejects unapproved expansion into crash recovery, sandboxing, observability exporters, or cross-package deduplication.
Final spec guard confirms every in-scope issue #593 criterion is implemented or explicitly approved for deferral.

processLibraryResearch.shapeSelected: Flat phase list with sparse breakpoints because the issue is a known feature/architecture bundle with identifiable runtime surfaces. A remaining-gap audit is first because current staging already contains substantial issue #593 implementation.
processLibraryResearch.repoPolicyApplied: Use agent tasks instead of kind:shell subtasks for direct Babysitter work in this repository; verification agents must run concrete commands and report exact command outputs.
